ucs.title: [LLM] Elderwood (Sneaky Panda / Beijing Group) watering-hole exploit chain: browser → child-proc → packed payload
ucs.description: Hunts Elderwood's signature 'Elderwood Platform' tradecraft: a strategic web compromise (watering hole) on a defense, NGO or human-rights-related site delivers an IE/Flash/Office client-side exploit (T1189 → T1203) which spawns a non-browser child process that drops and executes a packed/encoded binary (T1105 + T1027.002). The combination of drive-by → exploitation → packed dropper from a browser parent on a high-value target population is the hallmark of the Aurora-lineage Elderwood toolkit.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_path) as process_path values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("iexplore.exe","chrome.exe","firefox.exe","msedge.exe","AcroRd32.exe","FlashPlayer*.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host process_id [| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\AppData\\*","*\\Temp\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*") AND Filesystem.file_name IN ("*.exe","*.dll","*.scr","*.tmp") by host Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.process_id | rename Filesystem.process_id as process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`] | join type=inner host [| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Web where Web.http_method="GET" AND (Web.url="*.swf*" OR Web.url="*ie_exploit*" OR Web.url="*xul*" OR Web.dest_category="watering_hole_target" OR Web.category IN ("NGO","HumanRights","DefenseSupplier")) by host Web.src Web.dest Web.url] | where firstTime > relative_time(now(),"-15m") | table firstTime host Processes.user parent_process_name process_name process file_name file_path Web.dest Web.url
ucs.kql:
let suspiciousChildren = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
let browserParents = dynamic(["iexplore.exe","chrome.exe","firefox.exe","msedge.exe","acrord32.exe","flashplayer.exe","flashplayerplugin.exe"]);
let exploitChild = DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(1d)
  | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (browserParents)
  | where FileName in~ (suspiciousChildren)
  | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, BrowserParent=InitiatingProcessFileName, ChildProc=FileName, ChildPID=ProcessId, ChildCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
let droppedBin = DeviceFileEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(1d)
  | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
  | where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".dll" or FileName endswith ".scr"
  | where FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\")
  | project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DroppedFile=FileName, DroppedPath=FolderPath, InitiatingProcessId, InitiatingProcessFileName, SHA256;
exploitChild
  | join kind=inner (droppedBin) on DeviceId
  | where DropTime between (Timestamp .. Timestamp + 5m)
  | join kind=leftouter (
      DeviceNetworkEvents
      | where Timestamp > ago(1d)
      | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (browserParents)
      | project DeviceId, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, NetTime=Timestamp
  ) on DeviceId
  | where NetTime between (Timestamp - 10m .. Timestamp)
  | extend ElderwoodChain = strcat(BrowserParent, " -> ", ChildProc, " -> ", DroppedFile)
  | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ElderwoodChain, ChildCmd, DroppedPath, SHA256, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP
ucs.rationale: Elderwood / Beijing Group is the cluster behind Operation Aurora and a long campaign of watering-hole attacks against defense suppliers, NGOs, and human-rights orgs (per Symantec's 'Elderwood Project' report). Their distinguishing signature is not generic phishing but rather the Elderwood Platform: serving a 0-day from a compromised legitimate site, having the browser exploit pivot to a LOLBin chi

ucs.title: [LLM] Elderwood spearphishing with packed/encoded archive payload to defense & NGO recipients
ucs.description: Detects Elderwood's secondary delivery vector (T1566.001/.002 → T1204.001/.002 → T1027.002/.013): a targeted email to defense, supply-chain, NGO or human-rights mailboxes carrying a link or archive/document that, on user execution, writes a high-entropy packed binary and beacons out. Tuned to the actor's preference for password-protected archives and exploit-laden Office/PDF lures dropping packed PE droppers.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime values(All_Email.src_user) as sender values(All_Email.file_name) as attachment values(All_Email.subject) as subject from datamodel=Email where All_Email.recipient_category IN ("defense","ngo","humanrights","supplychain","itservices") AND (All_Email.file_name IN ("*.zip","*.rar","*.7z","*.iso","*.lnk","*.doc","*.docx","*.xls","*.xlsx","*.pdf") OR All_Email.url="*") by All_Email.recipient All_Email.message_id | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Email)` | join type=inner recipient [| tstats `summariesonly` count values(Processes.process) as exec_cmd values(Processes.process_name) as exec_proc from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winrar.exe","7zg.exe","explorer.exe","outlook.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","acrord32.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe") by Processes.user host | rename Processes.user as recipient | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | join type=inner host [| tstats `summariesonly` count values(Filesystem.file_name) as dropped values(Filesystem.file_hash) as hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*","*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*") AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.exe" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.dll") by host] | where firstTime > relative_time(now(),"-2h") | table firstTime recipient sender subject attachment exec_proc exec_cmd dropped hash
ucs.kql:
let targetGroups = dynamic(["Defense","NGO","HumanRights","SupplyChain","ITServices","R&D"]);
let riskyAttach = dynamic([".zip",".rar",".7z",".iso",".img",".lnk",".doc",".docm",".xls",".xlsm",".pdf",".chm",".hlp"]);
let officeOrArchiveParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","acrord32.exe","outlook.exe","winrar.exe","7zg.exe","explorer.exe"]);
let susChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe"]);
let phishMail = EmailEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(2d)
  | where DeliveryAction == "Delivered"
  | join kind=inner (EmailAttachmentInfo | where FileName has_any (riskyAttach)) on NetworkMessageId
  | extend RecipientLower = tolower(RecipientEmailAddress)
  | project MailTime=Timestamp, NetworkMessageId, SenderFromAddress, RecipientLower, Subject, FileName, SHA256;
let userExec = DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(2d)
  | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (officeOrArchiveParents)
  | where FileName in~ (susChildren)
  | extend AccountLower = tolower(strcat(AccountName, "@", AccountDomain))
  | project ExecTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountLower, Parent=InitiatingProcessFileName, Child=FileName, ChildCmd=ProcessCommandLine, ChildPID=ProcessId;
let packedDrop = DeviceFileEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(2d)
  | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
  | where FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\")
  | where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".dll"
  | project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DroppedName=FileName, DroppedPath=FolderPath, DroppedSHA256=SHA256, InitiatingProcessId;
phishMail
  | join kind=inner (userExec) on $left.RecipientLower == $right.AccountLower
  | where ExecTime between (MailTime .. MailTime + 6h)
  | join kind=inner (packedDrop) on DeviceId
  | where DropTime between (ExecTime .. ExecTime + 10m)
  | extend ElderwoodPhishChain = strcat(SenderFromAddress, " -> ", FileName, " -> ", Parent, "/", Child, " -> ", DroppedName)
  | project MailTime, ExecTime, DropTime, DeviceName, RecipientLower, SenderFromAddress, Subject, FileName, ElderwoodPhishChain, ChildCmd, DroppedPath, DroppedSHA256
ucs.rationale: Beyond watering holes, Elderwood (Beijing Group / Sneaky Panda) is documented sending tailored spearphish into the same victim verticals — defense supply chain, IT service providers, NGOs and human-rights groups — using exploit-laden Office docs and packed archive droppers (the same Elderwood Platform packers seen in Aurora and the Hydraq/Briba/Naid families). Pivoting from a recipient categorized
